q: Last weekend we ____ to the national park to look for wild deer. (drive)
The hunter ____ at the tiger but luckily he missed it. (shoot)
The eagle ____ high above the mountains and then disappeared. (fly)
We ____ a huge brown bear near the river yesterday morning. (see)
While we were camping, a fox ____ quietly past our tent. (creep)
The monkeys ____ from tree to tree when the storm began. (jump)
I saw a snake while I ____ walking through the long grass. (be)
The baby kangaroo ____ in its mother's warm pouch when we found them. (sleep)
Scientists have ____ this rare frog for many years now. (study)
That wolf has ____ in this forest since it was a cub. (live)
Have you ____ touched a real wild snake?
There ____ to be many wild elephants in this jungle, but now they are gone. (use)
This river ____ to be much cleaner before the factory was built. (use)
A cheetah is much ____ than a lion when it chases its prey. (fast)
The blue whale is the ____ animal that has ever lived on Earth. (large)
A young deer can run almost as ____ as an adult one.
If we cut down the rainforest, thousands of animals ____ lose their homes.
If you ____ very still, the wild rabbit will not run away.
Look at those dark clouds, I think it ____ going to rain on our safari. (be)
Next summer we ____ travel to Africa to watch the lions.
There isn't ____ water left in the lake during the dry season.
How ____ different species of birds live in this forest?
The cave was ____ dark for us to see the bats inside.
A panda is an animal ____ eats only bamboo all day long.
This is the forest ____ the last wild tigers still live.
You ____ not feed the wild monkeys because it makes them aggressive.
Visitors ____ to stay inside their cars during the lion safari.
An animal that only comes out at night and sleeps in the day is described as ____.
An animal that is in serious danger of disappearing forever is described as ____.
We have already ____ five different kinds of wild birds this morning.
Last year we ____ the New Year festival at my grandmother's house in the countryside. (celebrate)
The children ____ colourful lanterns down the river during the evening ceremony. (float)
My father ____ a traditional costume to the wedding because it was a very special day. (wear)
We ____ fireworks in the sky until midnight on the last night of the festival. (see)
While the parade ____ passing through the street, everyone was clapping and cheering. (pass)
At eight o'clock the cooks ____ a huge feast for all the guests. (prepare)
My sister was dancing in the square while the musicians ____ drums. (play)
We had already ____ the whole house before the guests arrived. (decorate)
I have known about this ancient tradition ____ I was a little child.
Have you ____ been to a carnival in another country?
People ____ to light huge bonfires on this hill, but now it is forbidden.
In the past my grandparents ____ to celebrate the harvest with the whole village.
This year's festival was much ____ than last year's because more visitors came. (big)
The drummers in the parade were the ____ performers of the whole show. (loud)
The fireworks display was as ____ as a real painting in the night sky.
If it ____ tomorrow, the outdoor festival will be moved into the hall. (rain)
If we leave home early, we ____ get good seats for the parade.
Look at those dark clouds; I think it is ____ to snow during the celebration.
Next Saturday we ____ visiting the traditional craft market with our class.
There were so ____ people at the festival that we could hardly move.
We didn't buy much food because there wasn't ____ money left in my pocket.
The hall was ____ small to hold everyone, so some guests waited outside.
A carnival is a festival ____ people wear masks and dance in the streets.
The woman ____ organised the whole ceremony is my aunt.
You ____ to bring a gift if you are invited to a traditional wedding here.
If you want to enjoy the festival, you ____ arrive before the parade starts.
A ____ is a custom passed down from older people to younger ones over many years.
The streets were beautifully ____ with flags and lights for the national holiday. (decorate)
During the festival the dancers moved more ____ than I had expected. (graceful)
Bright ____ exploded in the night sky with loud bangs and brilliant colours.
Last weekend we ____ the whole house before the guests arrived. (clean)
She ____ the dishes after dinner and then watched television. (wash)
My dad ____ a new wardrobe for the bedroom last month. (buy)
We ____ all the old furniture into the garage yesterday.
My grandmother ____ us her old clock when she moved house. (give)
While I was vacuuming the carpet, my brother ____ making his bed. (be)
They were ____ the kitchen floor when the doorbell rang. (mop)
At eight o'clock last night my parents were ____ in the living room. (relax)
I have already ____ the rubbish out this morning. (take)
We have lived in this flat ____ five years now.
My sister hasn't tidied her bedroom ____ .
My parents have ____ painted the kitchen since last spring. (paint)
There ____ to be an old fireplace in the corner of this room.
We didn't ____ to have a dishwasher in our old house.
The kitchen is the ____ room in our whole house. (big)
My bedroom is ____ than my brother's bedroom. (tidy)
The new sofa is just as ____ as the old armchair.
If you ____ the table, I will wash all the plates.
If it ____ tomorrow, we will paint the bedroom indoors. (rain)
Look at those dark clouds, I think it is ____ to rain.
I ____ help you move that heavy cupboard later this afternoon.
There isn't ____ space in the cupboard for all these towels.
There are too ____ chairs around the small dining table.
We have a ____ of furniture in the new living room.
The room ____ I sleep in has a big window facing the garden.
That is the neighbour ____ helped us carry the wardrobe upstairs.
You ____ to make your bed every morning before school.
You ____ tidy your room before your friends come over.
The piece of furniture you sit on with your family in the living room is a ____ .
The room where you cook and prepare meals is called the ____ .
Yesterday Tom felt so nervous that he ____ his whole speech. (forget)
She was furious and ____ the door behind her when she left. (slam)
When I told him the good news, he ____ with joy. (cry)
My grandfather was a calm man who never ____ his temper. (lose)
Yesterday she was so embarrassed that she ____ bright red. (go)
While I was waiting for my results, my hands ____ shaking. (be)
The shy boy was ____ at the floor while everyone stared at him. (look)
They were arguing loudly because both of them ____ feeling jealous. (be)
I have been anxious about this exam ____ Monday.
She has always ____ a very generous person. (be)
Have you ____ met someone as confident as my sister?
I have already ____ him three times, but he still hasn't replied. (call)
When he was younger he ____ to be extremely lazy, but now he works hard.
I ____ to get angry easily, but I'm much calmer these days.
My brother is far ____ than me when it comes to talking to strangers. (brave)
She is the ____ person in our class; she always shares her sweets. (generous)
He isn't as ____ as he pretends to be; deep down he's quite shy.
If you keep being so rude, nobody ____ want to be your friend.
If she apologises politely, he ____ probably forgive her.
Look how pale she is; I think she ____ going to faint.
Don't worry, I ____ help you calm down before the interview.
He's so impatient that he never has ____ patience to wait in queues.
There were ____ many people at the party that she felt overwhelmed.
She doesn't have ____ confidence to speak in front of the class.
That's the friend ____ always cheers me up when I'm sad.
Kindness is a quality ____ everybody admires in a person.
You look exhausted; you ____ to get some rest before you collapse.
He's been quiet all day, so something ____ be bothering him.
My sister never lifts a finger at home; she is incredibly ____ .
A ____ person always says please and thank you and never interrupts.
Last summer we ____ on a long road trip across the mountains. (go)
When the bus arrived, everyone ____ their bags and climbed on board. (grab)
The explorers ____ the ancient cave for the very first time. (find)
We ____ our tents near the river before it got dark.
When the rain started, we ____ running for shelter under the trees. (begin)
At eight o'clock last night they were still ____ for the lost compass. (look)
The children were ____ photos of the waterfall when the guide called them. (take)
I dropped my map because I ____ carrying too many things at once. (be)
We have ____ in this jungle for three days and still haven't reached the camp. (be)
Have you ever ____ a real adventure abroad?
They haven't ____ the top of the mountain yet, but they are close. (reach)
We have already ____ all our snacks, so we need to buy more food. (eat)
My brother ____ to be afraid of flying, but now he loves planes.
There ____ to be a small wooden bridge here before the storm destroyed it.
This trail is far ____ than the one we took yesterday. (steep)
Climbing at night was the ____ part of the whole expedition. (dangerous)
The second mountain was almost as ____ as the first one we climbed.
If it ____ tomorrow, we will cancel the boat trip. (rain)
If we leave early, we ____ catch the first ferry to the island.
Next week we ____ going to explore the old castle on the hill.
Hurry up, the train ____ leaving in five minutes and we can't miss it.
We didn't bring ____ water, so we got really thirsty on the climb.
There were too ____ tourists at the viewpoint, so we couldn't see much.
How ____ time do we have before the guided tour starts?
This is the village ____ we stayed during our first night of the trip.
The guide ____ led us through the forest was very experienced.
You ____ to carry a torch when you walk through the dark tunnel.
You ____ pack warm clothes because the nights in the mountains are cold.
A person who travels around to visit interesting places on holiday is a ____.
A bag that you carry on your back while hiking is called a ____.
